channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Who is Andy Burnham, the U.K.'s new prime minister?
item.description: On Monday, former Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham succeeds Keir Starmer as the United Kingdom's prime minister. Burnham's politics have been shaped by England's north-south class divide.
item.content:encoded:
On Monday, former Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham succeeds Keir Starmer as the United Kingdom's prime minister. Burnham's politics have been shaped by England's north-south class divide.

(Image credit: Carl Court)
item.dc:creator: Lauren Frayer

item.title: An Israeli paraglider tries to save migrating swifts that nest in the Western Wall
item.description: Swifts have nested in Jerusalem's Western Wall since biblical times. Now one Israeli paraglider is trying to save them.
item.content:encoded:
Swifts have nested in Jerusalem's Western Wall since biblical times. Now one Israeli paraglider is trying to save them.

(Image credit: Ruth Sherlock)
item.dc:creator: Ruth Sherlock

item.title: Deadly overnight Russian attack on Ukraine targets the capital
item.description: Russia launched 41 missiles and 125 attack drones across Ukraine overnight, according to the Ukrainian air force.
item.content:encoded:
Russia launched 41 missiles and 125 attack drones across Ukraine overnight, according to the Ukrainian air force.

(Image credit: Efrem Lukatsky)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Cuban artist and dissident exiled from country after 5 years in prison arrives in US
item.description: A famous Cuban dissident artist and musician, Luis Manuel Otero Alcántara, arrived in Miami on Saturday after being released from a five-year prison sentence on the condition that he leave his country.
item.content:encoded:
A famous Cuban dissident artist and musician, Luis Manuel Otero Alcántara, arrived in Miami on Saturday after being released from a five-year prison sentence on the condition that he leave his country.

(Image credit: David Santiago)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Andrew and Tristan Tate arrested in Florida on charges of rape and sex trafficking
item.description: Influencer Andrew Tate and his brother Tristan have been arrested in Miami after British authorities issued 38 new charges against them, and said they are "seeking extradition" for the two men.
item.content:encoded:
Influencer Andrew Tate and his brother Tristan have been arrested in Miami after British authorities issued 38 new charges against them, and said they are "seeking extradition" for the two men.

(Image credit: Julia Demaree Nikhinson)
item.dc:creator: Robbie Griffiths

item.title: Josh Kerr of Britain breaks 27-year-old world record in the mile
item.description: Kerr announced his bid to break the world record months in advance, and pulled off the plan in London on Saturday.
item.content:encoded:
Kerr announced his bid to break the world record months in advance, and pulled off the plan in London on Saturday.

(Image credit: Harry Murphy)
item.dc:creator: Huo Jingnan

item.title: U.S. launches new airstrikes to 'punish' Iran for troop deaths
item.description: Three U.S. service members were killed in action while "partner forces defended against Iranian ballistic missile and drone attacks," U.S. Central Command said Saturday.
item.content:encoded:
Three U.S. service members were killed in action while "partner forces defended against Iranian ballistic missile and drone attacks," U.S. Central Command said Saturday.

(Image credit: Vahid Salemi)
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster

item.title: Why is it so hard for the U.S. to win wars?
item.description: U.S. presidents have promised short, decisive wars in Afghanistan, Iraq and Iran. All have proved much more difficult than advertised and fallen far short of the political goals set at the beginning.
item.content:encoded:
U.S. presidents have promised short, decisive wars in Afghanistan, Iraq and Iran. All have proved much more difficult than advertised and fallen far short of the political goals set at the beginning.

(Image credit: Brennan Linsley)
item.dc:creator: Greg Myre

item.title: 8 killed and more than 60 wounded in Ukrainian drone attack on Russian regions
item.description: Kyiv's forces are continuing their aerial campaign against energy infrastructure and military targets inside Russia, aiming to undermine Moscow's war effort.
item.content:encoded:
Kyiv's forces are continuing their aerial campaign against energy infrastructure and military targets inside Russia, aiming to undermine Moscow's war effort.

(Image credit: Moscow Region Governor Andrei Vo)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: The U.S. and Iran blow past red lines as they lurch back toward all-out war
item.description: The United States and Iran exchanged strikes aimed at infrastructure and military targets on Saturday as their battle over the Strait of Hormuz intensified.
item.content:encoded:
The United States and Iran exchanged strikes aimed at infrastructure and military targets on Saturday as their battle over the Strait of Hormuz intensified.

(Image credit: Razieh Poudat/AP Photo)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
